guides.title: Annual Career Review System
guides.excerpt: Actionable framework to improve decision quality and execution in career development.
guides.body_md:
# Annual Career Review System

## Why This Matters
Career growth is a compound system. Better decisions early reduce switching costs and improve long-term optionality.

## Practical Framework
1. Define one 12-month target role with explicit success criteria.
2. Build a capability backlog across knowledge, projects, and collaboration behavior.
3. Run 4-week execution sprints and collect measurable evidence.
4. Review outcomes quarterly and adjust strategy using market and fit signals.

## Implementation Checklist
- Clear role target and industry context
- Evidence-based skill gap map
- Portfolio or project proof
- Feedback loop with mentors and stakeholders

guides.title: INTJ Career Playbook: Turn Strategic Judgment Into Trusted Influence
guides.excerpt: For INTJs who already think in systems and need a clearer path from private judgment to visible, trusted career leverage.
guides.body_md:
# INTJ Career Playbook

## Why this guide fits INTJ
INTJs usually do not lack judgment. The real bottleneck is making strong thinking easier for other people to understand, trust, and act on.

## Where INTJs usually win
- Long-range planning and system design
- Complex tradeoff judgment under ambiguity
- Building structures that keep working after the first launch

## Next-step moves
1. Explain the frame before the conclusion in high-stakes conversations.
2. Publish one visible artifact each quarter: memo, roadmap, architecture note, or decision model.
3. Pair deep-work blocks with a repeatable stakeholder alignment rhythm.

guides.title: INTP Career Playbook: Let Depth Travel All the Way to Execution
guides.excerpt: For INTPs who think deeply and need a better bridge from elegant models to shipped work, influence, and real-world feedback.
guides.body_md:
# INTP Career Playbook

## Why this guide fits INTP
INTPs often see the model before others do. Career growth accelerates when that depth reaches a usable recommendation, tool, prototype, or published point of view.

## Where INTPs usually win
- Abstract reasoning and model building
- Pattern detection across messy information
- Improving methods, frameworks, and systems

## Next-step moves
1. Convert one core idea into a prototype, memo, or test every cycle.
2. Share unfinished thinking with a small review loop before it becomes too private.
3. Practice closing with a recommendation, not only an analysis.

guides.title: ENTJ Career Playbook: Scale Leadership Beyond Personal Output
guides.excerpt: For ENTJs who already move fast and need to turn strong personal execution into systems, delegation, and sustainable team trust.
guides.body_md:
# ENTJ Career Playbook

## Why this guide fits ENTJ
ENTJs usually do not struggle with ambition. The bigger question is whether leadership is still running through personal willpower or has started to scale through people and systems.

## Where ENTJs usually win
- Direction setting and decision clarity
- Resource coordination under pressure
- Translating goals into execution structures

## Next-step moves
1. Define what only you should own and what should become delegated process.
2. Review whether team pace is sustainable, not only whether targets are moving.
3. Build a stronger feedback loop with peers and direct reports before friction becomes attrition.

guides.title: ENTP Career Playbook: Turn Idea Density Into Shippable Momentum
guides.excerpt: For ENTPs who generate options fast and need a stronger habit of turning sharp ideas into trusted experiments, results, and reputation.
guides.body_md:
# ENTP Career Playbook

## Why this guide fits ENTP
ENTPs often have more ideas than any single quarter can hold. Career leverage grows when curiosity is paired with enough delivery discipline to make the best ideas real.

## Where ENTPs usually win
- Reframing stale problems from a fresh angle
- Connecting strategy, communication, and experimentation
- Challenging assumptions without losing speed

## Next-step moves
1. Choose fewer ideas and take more of them to a visible end state.
2. Pair your exploratory energy with a reliable finisher or review loop.
3. Soften delivery, not logic, when you need others to stay with you.

guides.title: INFJ Career Playbook: Protect Meaning While Building Real Influence
guides.excerpt: For INFJs who care about depth, people, and direction, and need a steadier way to turn insight into visible professional impact.
guides.body_md:
# INFJ Career Playbook

## Why this guide fits INFJ
INFJs often know what matters before they know how to say it. Career growth becomes easier when inner clarity is translated into a role, boundary, and contribution that others can actually see.

## Where INFJs usually win
- Reading deeper patterns in people and systems
- Connecting values with long-range direction
- Creating thoughtful support, guidance, and trust

## Next-step moves
1. Name the kind of impact you want instead of waiting for the right title to appear.
2. Build boundaries around emotional labor before it becomes invisible over-responsibility.
3. Practice sharing unfinished insight earlier in the process.

guides.title: INFP Career Playbook: Keep Values and Craft on the Same Side
guides.excerpt: For INFPs who want work to feel honest, meaningful, and creatively alive without losing structure, income, or momentum.
guides.body_md:
# INFP Career Playbook

## Why this guide fits INFP
INFPs usually thrive when work feels aligned with both inner values and personal craft. Motivation collapses when output becomes empty performance or constant external pressure.

## Where INFPs usually win
- Meaningful storytelling and creative interpretation
- Quiet empathy and one-to-one understanding
- Work that improves experience, care, or expression

## Next-step moves
1. Define your non-negotiables for meaning before choosing the next role.
2. Build a simple execution rhythm so inspiration has somewhere to land.
3. Let your portfolio show what you care about, not only what you can do.

guides.title: ENFJ Career Playbook: Lead People Without Carrying Everyone
guides.excerpt: For ENFJs who energize people naturally and need better boundaries, pacing, and role clarity so care does not turn into overload.
guides.body_md:
# ENFJ Career Playbook

## Why this guide fits ENFJ
ENFJs often become the emotional center of a team before anyone formally asks them to. Career growth becomes more sustainable when care is paired with boundaries, structure, and role clarity.

## Where ENFJs usually win
- Creating trust and momentum in groups
- Turning values into communication and direction
- Coaching, coordination, and people-facing leadership

## Next-step moves
1. Separate support from over-responsibility in every key relationship.
2. Make people-development work visible instead of letting it stay invisible labor.
3. Protect recovery time as part of performance, not as an optional reward.

guides.title: ENFP Career Playbook: Keep Freedom While Protecting Delivery
guides.excerpt: For ENFPs who create energy, ideas, and connection easily but need a stronger closed-loop rhythm to turn momentum into career capital.
guides.body_md:
# ENFP Career Playbook

## Why this guide fits ENFP
ENFPs usually do not need help generating motion. The more useful upgrade is learning how to focus that motion long enough to create visible work, trust, and repeat opportunity.

## Where ENFPs usually win
- Creative expression and idea generation
- Connecting people, stories, and possibilities
- Energizing new communities, products, or initiatives

## Next-step moves
1. Limit active priorities so your best energy reaches the finish line.
2. Protect exploratory time by anchoring it to one stable output rhythm.
3. Build a personal archive of shipped work, not only inspiring conversations.

guides.title: ISTJ Career Playbook: Stay Reliable Without Getting Stuck
guides.excerpt: For ISTJs who already deliver steady value and need more visibility, flexibility, and role expansion without losing their standards.
guides.body_md:
# ISTJ Career Playbook

## Why this guide fits ISTJ
ISTJs are often trusted because they do what they say and keep systems stable. The next upgrade is making that reliability more visible and adaptable as scope grows.

## Where ISTJs usually win
- Process discipline and dependable execution
- Quality control, compliance, and operational follow-through
- Building routines people can count on

## Next-step moves
1. Explain the value of your work before others assume it is just maintenance.
2. Practice small flexibility in low-risk situations so change feels less disruptive.
3. Link accuracy to business impact when you communicate upward.

guides.title: ISFJ Career Playbook: Protect Care With Better Boundaries
guides.excerpt: For ISFJs who support people steadily and need clearer boundaries so reliability remains a strength instead of a hidden drain.
guides.body_md:
# ISFJ Career Playbook

## Why this guide fits ISFJ
ISFJs often become indispensable because they notice what others need before anyone asks. Career growth becomes healthier when care is paired with explicit boundaries and role recognition.

## Where ISFJs usually win
- Consistent support and follow-through
- Thoughtful service and people care
- Detail-sensitive execution in stable systems

## Next-step moves
1. Name which support tasks are truly part of your role.
2. Track invisible contributions so they can be recognized in reviews.
3. Build recovery habits before helpfulness turns into exhaustion.

guides.title: ESTJ Career Playbook: Upgrade Strong Execution Into Durable Leadership
guides.excerpt: For ESTJs who already deliver hard results and need a better balance of authority, flexibility, and team sustainability.
guides.body_md:
# ESTJ Career Playbook

## Why this guide fits ESTJ
ESTJs usually know how to create order and get results. The next level is learning when to push harder, when to adapt, and how to keep standards high without over-controlling the system.

## Where ESTJs usually win
- Clear structure, accountability, and pace
- Operational consistency and decision speed
- Protecting execution quality under pressure

## Next-step moves
1. Separate essential standards from personal preferences.
2. Build room for feedback before problems become resistance.
3. Train successors and systems so performance does not depend on your constant intervention.

guides.title: ESFJ Career Playbook: Turn Care Into Professional Influence
guides.excerpt: For ESFJs who support teams and customers generously and need clearer boundaries so warmth becomes recognized professional value.
guides.body_md:
# ESFJ Career Playbook

## Why this guide fits ESFJ
ESFJs are often the people others trust first. Career growth becomes stronger when care, reliability, and service are treated as professional capabilities rather than assumed emotional labor.

## Where ESFJs usually win
- Relationship maintenance and practical support
- Service quality and team coordination
- Building trust through consistency and responsiveness

## Next-step moves
1. Name the operational value behind your people work.
2. Stop rescuing every process gap by yourself.
3. Protect a few non-negotiable boundaries around energy and time.

guides.title: ISTP Career Playbook: Make Technical Strength More Visible and Durable
guides.excerpt: For ISTPs who solve real problems well and need a stronger way to make skill, judgment, and long-term direction more visible.
guides.body_md:
# ISTP Career Playbook

## Why this guide fits ISTP
ISTPs often gain trust by fixing what other people cannot. Career ceilings rise when technical skill is paired with stronger visibility, collaboration, and a more deliberate map for what comes next.

## Where ISTPs usually win
- Hands-on troubleshooting and calm decision making
- Rapid learning through direct experimentation
- Independent work with clear technical feedback

## Next-step moves
1. Describe the value behind your technical fixes, not only the fix itself.
2. Build enough relationship trust that autonomy does not look like disengagement.
3. Choose one future-facing capability to deepen before urgency chooses it for you.

guides.title: ISFP Career Playbook: Let Your Work Be Seen Without Losing Your Tone
guides.excerpt: For ISFPs who create with sincerity and taste, and need a gentler way to make work visible, valued, and sustainable over time.
guides.body_md:
# ISFP Career Playbook

## Why this guide fits ISFP
ISFPs often do their best work quietly, through craft, atmosphere, and care. Career momentum grows when that quality is made easier to notice without forcing a louder identity than feels true.

## Where ISFPs usually win
- Taste, feeling, and experience sensitivity
- Creating work that is gentle, usable, and human
- Craft that improves everyday life in tangible ways

## Next-step moves
1. Help your work get seen before assuming it will speak entirely for itself.
2. Protect creative energy with firmer boundaries around requests.
3. Add a light long-term plan so freedom does not become drift.

guides.title: ESTP Career Playbook: Pair Fast Action With Better Risk Framing
guides.excerpt: For ESTPs who move quickly in the real world and need a stronger habit of turning instinct, momentum, and nerve into durable judgment.
guides.body_md:
# ESTP Career Playbook

## Why this guide fits ESTP
ESTPs often create opportunity by acting while others are still hesitating. Career growth gets stronger when speed is paired with enough framing to reduce avoidable risk and make wins repeatable.

## Where ESTPs usually win
- Fast action in uncertain real-world settings
- Reading people, pressure, and timing in the moment
- Closing gaps between intention and execution

## Next-step moves
1. Slow down just enough to define the risk before the jump.
2. Capture what is working so instinct becomes repeatable playbook.
3. Pair adrenaline-heavy work with one longer-term capability build.

guides.title: ESFP Career Playbook: Keep Energy High While Building Lasting Skill
guides.excerpt: For ESFPs who thrive in live, people-rich environments and want to turn presence, warmth, and improvisation into durable career growth.
guides.body_md:
# ESFP Career Playbook

## Why this guide fits ESFP
ESFPs often shine where there is energy, immediacy, and real human response. Career growth becomes stronger when that live strength is paired with a little more planning, boundary, and skill accumulation.

## Where ESFPs usually win
- Creating atmosphere and human connection in the moment
- Responding fluidly to changing situations
- Turning service, performance, or experience into real loyalty

## Next-step moves
1. Keep the spontaneity, but anchor it to one simple planning habit.
2. Decide what you will and will not absorb from other people’s moods.
3. Build one durable skill behind the visible charisma.
